spec_match.rationale: The page calls PaymentRequest.getSecurePaymentConfirmationCapabilities when exposed and uses the result to gate biometric SPC versus card fallback paths.

interactive.rationale: The repaired four-step checkout, device scenarios, real capability probe, apply action, payment selection, confirmation, and reset controls all update visible state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The simulator delivers the capability-gated checkout and graceful fallback flow promised by the feature page.

edge_cases.rationale: It covers SPC supported, no platform authenticator, missing API, thrown/disabled capability calls, card fallback, back navigation, and restart.

references.rationale: The page links ChromeStatus feature 4727235745546240 and the Secure Payment Confirmation capabilities specification.

conformance.rationale: The live feature route passes all 5 assertions for PaymentRequest, the static capability method, its Promise return, credentials exposure, and payment credential support.

runtime_evidence.rationale: DevTools MCP reproduced the broken goStep inline call, then exercised the full simulated checkout, fallback scenario, reset, and real API error path with clean console output after repair.

telemetry_health.rationale: Unsupported API state is caught and rendered as text; post-repair interactions produce no unexpected runtime, console, resource, or assertion failures.

design_system.rationale: The multi-step flow uses shared design tokens, clear badges and text, and a real control surface rather than static code cards.

accessibility.rationale: The device selector now has a precise name, native buttons/radios support keyboard operation, payment inputs are labelled, and each step exposes its result through readable text.

summary: The sweep found every checkout navigation button depended on inline calls to the IIFE-scoped goStep function, so the primary flow failed immediately. Inline handlers were replaced with data-step and explicit listeners, dynamic payment radios use delegated change handling, and reset/complete/scenario actions are wired directly. DevTools now completes the full four-step flow and graceful real-probe failure path; conformance passes 5/5.
